rulings.Case Title: GREGG RANERI VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Dillingham Construction, N.A., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Charles Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to paragraph 7 of plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled as to paragraphs 23 and 25. Since the Court finds that Mr. Ay is an expert on insulation, the Court did not need to decide his expertise on gaskets. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL BRUMETT VS. GEORGIA PACIFIC CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant The W.W. Henry Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, defendant's separate statement fails to lay out undisputed facts demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the Henry's mastic to which he was exposed contained asbestos. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Plaintiff's objections to Lori Angelo's deposition testimony are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND TOWNSEND et al VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Day & Zimmermann, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication issues 1, 2 and 3, defendant sustained its initial burden. Decedent's deposition testimony and Mr. Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Decedent's deposition testimony is admissible because decedent is unavailable, defendant was a party to decedent's personal injury action and defendant had the right and opportunity to cross-examine decedent with an interest and motive similar to that which it has in this action. (Evid. Code § 1291.) Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled. Summary adjudication of issue 4 (punitive damages) is denied as moot because plaintiffs do not make such claim against defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
